title: Tomb of Two Kamals
summary:
The Tomb of Two Kamals (Persian: آرامگاه دو کمال), or in short the 2-Kamal Tomb, is a funerary monument in Tabriz, Iran. Two Kamals – the 14th-century poet Kamal Khujandi and the 15th-century miniaturist Kamal ed-Din Behzad – are buried in an underground chamber below a simple stone dome. The mausoleum also houses the remains of other artists and poets. It was built in the Safavid era as part of a larger cemetery, but deteriorated over the next centuries and was only restored in 1959. In the 2010s, a school was built around the mausoleum, which houses classrooms, a museum, a library, and other places which commemorate the legacies of the artists buried therein.


== References ==
